context_update.summary: This chunk contains the front matter of the paper, including title, authors, affiliations, abstract, and the beginning of the introduction. The abstract provides key facts about the designed Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni (wt.%) duplex lightweight steel: it consists of austenite and B2 phases with dual nanoprecipitates (L'1_2-type long-range ordered domains and VC carbides) within austenite. Mechanical properties are YS 1316±16 MPa, UTS 1458±11 MPa, and TEL 11.7±1.2%. Strengthening is attributed to HDI, solid solution, and precipitation mechanisms; ductility originates from substantial dislocation accumulation and HDI hardening at phase boundaries. The introduction reviews limitations of κ'-carbides, benefits of LRO domains and VC precipitation, and the advantages of heterogeneous structures with B2 phase. The present study aims to combine LRO- and VC-strengthened austenite with a hard B2 phase via a simple route of cold rolling and heat treatment. No detailed processing parameters, microstructural quantifications, or figures/tables are provided in this chunk. Subsequent sections are expected to describe materials and methods, initial microstructures, and mechanical properties in detail.
processing_route: cold rolling
heat treatment
simple processing route (details not yet given)
microstructure: duplex structure of austenite and B2
L'1_2 long-range ordered domains in austenite
VC carbides in austenite
mechanisms: dislocation accumulation in both austenite and B2

context_update.summary: This chunk covers the Materials and Methods section, detailing alloy processing, tensile testing, and characterization. The Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni steel ingot’s measured composition is given in Table 1. Processing involves homogenization at 1050 °C/1 h, hot rolling to 3 mm then water quenching, cold rolling to 1.5 mm (single-pass ≤5%, total 50% reduction), and solution treatment at 900 °C/15 min followed by water quenching (Fig. 1a). Equilibrium phase diagram (Fig. 1b) shows austenite and B2 phases with VC precipitation at 900 °C. Tensile specimens gauge 20×5×1.5 mm³ are tested at 1×10⁻³ s⁻¹; LUR tests use same rate. In situ SEM/EBSD tensile tests use specimens gauge 8×2×1 mm³ at 2 µm/s, paused at 0, 1, 3, 6% strain. XRD parameters: Cu-Kα, 20–100°, 2°/min. EBSD settings: tilt 70°, 20 kV, WD 15 mm, step 0.1 µm. GND density calculated via MATLAB. TEM foils thinned to <100 µm, then jet-polished at -30 °C, 20 V in 90% methanol-10% perchloric acid. TEM operated at 200 kV with STEM-EDX. Later sections will build on these processing and characterization parameters to discuss microstructure and properties.
processing_route: Homogenization at 1050 °C for 1 h
Hot rolling to 3 mm then water quenching
Cold rolling with 50% total reduction (single-pass ≤5%) to 1.5 mm
Solution treatment at 900 °C for 15 min followed by water quenching
key_variables: tensile gauge dimensions 20×5×1.5 mm³ and 8×2×1 mm³
in situ tensile rate 2 µm/s interruption strains 0/1/3/6%
EBSD tilt 70° 20 kV WD 15 mm step 0.1 µm
TEM foil <100 µm jet-polishing -30 °C 20 V 90% methanol 10% perchloric acid
microstructure: Equilibrium phases at 900 °C: austenite + B2 + VC

context_update.summary: This chunk presents the initial microstructure of the Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel after solution treatment. XRD confirmed the presence of austenite, B2, and VC precipitates and quantified the B2 phase fraction as 20%. EBSD revealed a duplex microstructure composed of equiaxed austenite grains with an average size of 1.31 µm and B2 phase appearing as both long strip-like large grains and fine particles along austenite grain boundaries. The B2 phase exhibits a bimodal size distribution with mean grain sizes of 1.02 µm and 3.38 µm. TEM analyses provided detailed precipitation and nano-domain characteristics: VC nanoprecipitates have an average size of 19.9 nm, a volume fraction of 0.26%, and a coherent interface with austenite. L'1_2 long-range ordered domains are homogeneously distributed nanoscale particles with irregular shapes, an average size of 1.0 nm, and a volume fraction of 11.10%. B2 forms a semi-coherent interface with austenite. Elemental partitioning shows Ni and Al enrichment in B2 and V and C enrichment in VC. These quantified microstructural features (phase fraction, grain sizes, precipitate sizes, volume fractions) serve as the baseline for interpreting mechanical properties and deformation microstructure evolution in the upcoming sections (3.2 and 3.3). Key figures include Figure 2 (XRD, EBSD maps, grain-size distributions) and Figure 3 (STEM-EDS, HRTEM, LRO domain images).
processing_route: Homogenization at 1050 °C for 1 h
Hot rolling to 3 mm + water quenching
Cold rolling 50% reduction to 1.5 mm (single-pass ≤5%)
Solution treatment at 900 °C for 15 min + water quenching
key_variables: B2 bimodal grain sizes: 1.02 µm, 3.38 µm
microstructure: Duplex microstructure of equiaxed austenite and B2 (strip-like grains + boundary particles)
VC nanoprecipitates coherent with austenite
L'1_2 LRO domains homogeneously distributed with irregular shapes
B2 semi-coherent with austenite

context_update.summary: Section 3.2 provides the mechanical properties of the Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel. Tensile tests (Figure 4) yield a yield strength of 1316±16 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 1458±11 MPa, and uniform elongation of 11.7±1.2%; the fracture surface shows dimples indicating ductile fracture. The work-hardening rate curve reveals three stages: stage I (0–1% true strain) drops sharply to negative, stage II (1–4.3%) increases rapidly, and stage III (>4.3%) decreases slowly but remains at a high level of 2.7–3.7 GPa. LUR tests (Figure 5) at 1–11% engineering strain quantify the Bauschinger effect: hysteresis loop area and Δσ_y increase with strain, and HDI stress derived as (σ_u+σ_r)/2 rises from 620 MPa at 1% true strain to 845 MPa at 11% true strain. The HDI strengthening mechanism involves GND accumulation at austenite/B2 interfaces. These macroscopic property data and strain-hardening characteristics will be linked to deformation microstructural evolution in subsequent sections.
processing_route: Homogenization 1050 °C/1 h
Hot rolling to 3 mm + water quenching
Cold rolling 50% reduction to 1.5 mm
Solution treatment 900 °C/15 min + water quenching
microstructure: Duplex austenite + B2 (20% B2, bimodal size distribution)
Austenite grain size ~1.31 µm
VC nanoprecipitates ~19.9 nm (0.26 vol%)
L'1_2 ordered domains ~1.0 nm (11.10 vol%)
Austenite/B2 semi-coherent interfaces
properties: Work-hardening stages: I (0-1%) drop below zero, II (1-4.3%) rapid increase, III (>4.3%) 2.7-3.7 GPa
HDI stress increases from 620 MPa at 1% to 845 MPa at 11% true strain
Hysteresis area and Δσ_y increase with strain (Figure 5c data)
mechanisms: HDI strengthening due to GND accumulation at austenite/B2 interfaces
Bauschinger effect reflected by hysteresis loops
figures_or_tables: Figure 5c (hysteresis area and Δσ_y vs. strain)
Tables embedded in Figure 4 and Figure 5 (stress-strain, work-hardening, LUR, HDI stress data)

context_update.summary: Section 3.3 characterizes the deformation microstructure evolution of the Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel using in situ EBSD and postmortem TEM. GND density maps at 0%, 1%, 3%, and 6% strain show co-deformation of austenite and B2. At 0%, initial GND density is higher in B2 due to its smaller grain size and residual dislocations from incomplete annealing at 900 °C. With strain, dislocation density in austenite rises faster and exceeds that of B2 at 6% strain. GND differences among grains with similar sizes are attributed to variations in Schmid factor. TEM at 6% strain reveals nonplanar slip bands in austenite, pile-ups near B2, shear-induced weakening and volume loss of LRO domains, and VC particles pinning dislocations. At 12% strain, dislocation density sharply increases; the austenite/B2 interface hinders slip and reflects dislocations, creating planar dislocation arrays in austenite. LRO volume continues shrinking and VC obstacle effect intensifies. These observations provide the microstructural basis for understanding the high strain hardening and the interplay of precipitation and interface strengthening discussed later. Key figures: Figure 5 (Bauschinger/HDI), Figure 6 (GND), Figure 7 (Schmid factor), Figure 8 (TEM).
processing_route: Homogenization at 1050°C/1h
hot rolling to 3 mm + water quenching
cold rolling 50% reduction
solution treatment at 900°C/15 min + water quenching
microstructure: duplex structure with equiaxed austenite and B2
initial GND: austenite 5.36×10^13 m^-2, B2 1.04×10^14 m^-2 at 0%
after 6% strain: austenite GND 2.64×10^14 m^-2, B2 2.51×10^14 m^-2
LRO domains sheared and weakened during deformation
VC precipitates pinning dislocations
austenite/B2 interface reflecting dislocations at high strain
mechanisms: Orowan-type bypass of VC particles leading to dislocation accumulation
HDI hardening from GND pile-ups at phase boundaries
interface dislocation reflection and planar array formation

rolling_context_summary:
Summary: Section 3.3 characterizes the deformation microstructure evolution of the Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel using in situ EBSD and postmortem TEM. GND density maps at 0%, 1%, 3%, and 6% strain show co-deformation of austenite and B2. At 0%, initial GND density is higher in B2 due to its smaller grain size and residual dislocations from incomplete annealing at 900 °C. With strain, dislocation density in austenite rises faster and exceeds that of B2 at 6% strain. GND differences among grains with similar sizes are attributed to variations in Schmid factor. TEM at 6% strain reveals nonplanar slip bands in austenite, pile-ups near B2, shear-induced weakening and volume loss of LRO domains, and VC particles pinning dislocations. At 12% strain, dislocation density sharply increases; the austenite/B2 interface hinders slip and reflects dislocations, creating planar dislocation arrays in austenite. LRO volume continues shrinking and VC obstacle effect intensifies. These observations provide the microstructural basis for understanding the high strain hardening and the interplay of precipitation and interface strengthening discussed later. Key figures: Figure 5 (Bauschinger/HDI), Figure 6 (GND), Figure 7 (Schmid factor), Figure 8 (TEM).
Material systems: Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni (wt.%) duplex lightweight steel; austenite (γ); B2 (NiAl); L'1_2-type long-range ordered domains; VC carbides; Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel; VC; L'1_2 ordered domains
Processing route: cold rolling; heat treatment; simple processing route (details not yet given); Homogenization at 1050 °C for 1 h; Hot rolling to 3 mm then water quenching; Cold rolling with 50% total reduction (single-pass ≤5%) to 1.5 mm; Solution treatment at 900 °C for 15 min followed by water quenching; Hot rolling to 3 mm + water quenching
Key variables: composition; phases: austenite, B2; precipitates: LRO domains, VC; yield strength; tensile strength; total elongation; strengthening mechanisms; deformation mechanisms
Microstructure: duplex structure of austenite and B2; L'1_2 long-range ordered domains in austenite; VC carbides in austenite; Equilibrium phases at 900 °C: austenite + B2 + VC; Duplex microstructure of equiaxed austenite and B2 (strip-like grains + boundary particles); VC nanoprecipitates coherent with austenite; L'1_2 LRO domains homogeneously distributed with irregular shapes; B2 semi-coherent with austenite
Properties: yield strength: 1316 ± 16 MPa; tensile strength: 1458 ± 11 MPa; total elongation: 11.7 ± 1.2%; YS 1316±16 MPa; UTS 1458±11 MPa; Uniform elongation 11.7±1.2%; Work-hardening stages: I (0-1%) drop below zero, II (1-4.3%) rapid increase, III (>4.3%) 2.7-3.7 GPa; HDI stress increases from 620 MPa at 1% to 845 MPa at 11% true strain
Mechanisms: HDI strengthening; solid solution strengthening; precipitation strengthening; dislocation accumulation in both austenite and B2; HDI hardening near grain/phase boundaries; HDI strengthening due to GND accumulation at austenite/B2 interfaces; Bauschinger effect reflected by hysteresis loops; dislocation shear of LRO domains

context_update.summary: This chunk covers the quantitative breakdown of yield strength and the analysis of work-hardening stages in the Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel. In Section 4.1, the total YS is decomposed into contributions from austenite, B2 phase, and HDI strengthening. Specific contributions are calculated: solid solution strengthening 228 MPa, dislocation forest strengthening 106 MPa (austenite) and 227 MPa (B2), VC precipitation 92 MPa, HDI stress 597 MPa, and LRO domain strengthening 119 MPa. Parameters for the Taylor model are given. Figure 9 and its data summarize the YS composition. Section 4.2 details three work-hardening stages: Stage I shows a sharp drop and negative rate due to interface relaxation; Stage II rises to a maximum near 4.3% true strain due to rapid dislocation storage and HDI hardening; Stage III maintains a high rate (>2.7 GPa) despite dynamic recovery and LRO dissolution, sustained by continuous interface dislocation accumulation. The Conclusions reiterate the excellent tensile properties (YS 1316±16 MPa, UTS 1458±11 MPa, EL 11.7±1.2%) and primary strengthening and ductility mechanisms. The chunk provides final quantitative strengthening contributions and strain hardening rationale essential for the overall understanding of the alloy’s performance.
processing_route: simple rolling and heat treatment (1050 °C/1h homogenization, hot rolling to 3 mm, cold rolling 50% to 1.5 mm, solution at 900 °C/15 min)
key_variables: stage II maximum near 4.3% true strain
microstructure: duplex structure: austenite + B2
L'1_2 LRO domains in austenite
VC nanoprecipitates in austenite
properties: work-hardening rate at stage III 2.7–3.7 GPa
mechanisms: HDI strengthening from dislocation pile-ups at interfaces (597 MPa)
stage I: dislocation free path, interface relaxation
stage II: dislocation storage and HDI hardening
stage III: dynamic recovery, LRO dissolution, sustained HDI hardening
